[2018-05-04 21:19] sunfallte (Sunfall): Those of you into Chip's Challenge should check out MESH.  http://kaser.com/mesh.html
[2018-05-04 21:20] jamesa7171: escape is another great one: http://escape.spacebar.org/
Escape : Free Puzzle game for Windows, Linux, Mac OSX
Escape is a deep tile-based puzzle game with internet features.
[2018-05-04 21:23] liadahlia (Lia): what is this monument thing i've been hearing about?
[2018-05-04 21:24] jamesa7171: having said that i've never found an optimization challenge as satisfying as cc regardless of the non-optimization gameplay of other games
[2018-05-04 21:24] jamesa7171: tinlun they're playing the campaign missions with a pretend extra unit that costs 1 gold and a drone, with the goal of buying that unit as early as possible and still beating the mission
[2018-05-04 21:25] jamesa7171: (with second purchase timing as tiebreak, and third purchase timing as additional tiebreak etc.)
[2018-05-04 21:25] mtanzer: buying a turn 1 monument is like playing the mission with 1 less drone from the start
[2018-05-04 21:25] mtanzer: the unit was MasN's idea for making the expert missions challenging to optimize
[2018-05-04 21:30] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): So he has been posting progress of a challenge that they set themselves to
[2018-05-04 21:31] mtanzer: yeah we did this with chapter 1 when it came out too, although some of us were optimizing for overkill then while masn was the one trying to do monument
[2018-05-04 21:32] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Oh I thought monument meant floating 1 gold from that point on.
[2018-05-04 21:32] jamesa7171: notes should be 6, not 5
[2018-05-04 21:33] http404error: I would leave out the line numbers anyways
[2018-05-04 21:33] Deleted User (Deleted User): Theres a lot of proofing to be done
[2018-05-04 21:33] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Strongly prefer unit names over unit icons
[2018-05-04 21:33] Deleted User (Deleted User): Even if limited to BSO?
[2018-05-04 21:34] mtanzer: there was another version of monument that worked that way.  I guess the 1 gold monument is even more extreme since it allows finer control, but I guess masn prefers the gold+drone one now
[2018-05-04 21:34] http404error: The icons are optional anyways
[2018-05-04 21:35] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Yeah, since you need words for non-BSO so it's weird to mix them.
[2018-05-04 21:35] http404error: I'm not sure what "Notes:" is supposed to encompass
[2018-05-04 21:35] Deleted User (Deleted User): Any additional information that doesnt fit in the other lines
[2018-05-04 21:35] supergreyman (Greyman): Notes: This unit is OP. 😄
[2018-05-04 21:35] Deleted User (Deleted User): As per the introduction unused lines are omitted
[2018-05-04 21:35] http404error: Are there any examples of that in Prismata?
[2018-05-04 21:36] supergreyman (Greyman): Campaign units have a plenty. 😛
[2018-05-04 21:36] Deleted User (Deleted User): In a unit design setting it may be necessary for the designer to convey additional information.
[2018-05-04 21:36] http404error: Sure, I don't think you need to specify a field for that specifically though
[2018-05-04 21:36] http404error: People will naturally put it at the end
[2018-05-04 21:37] http404error: I think I'm in favor of changing the first-line convention
[2018-05-04 21:38] http404error: I like grouping HP, fragile, and blocker
[2018-05-04 21:38] http404error: Still thinking about the ordering of cost/supply though
[2018-05-04 21:38] http404error: I think supply clearly goes in parens
[2018-05-04 21:38] http404error: But it might be better after cost
[2018-05-04 21:38] http404error: I do think (cost, supply) is not ideal
[2018-05-04 21:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): The () should be limited to supply
[2018-05-04 21:39] http404error: **Tarsier** 4R (10 supply)
**Tarsier** (10 supply) 4R
[2018-05-04 21:40] http404error: **Tarsier** 4:Gold::Red: (10 supply)
**Tarsier** (10 supply) 4:Gold::Red:
[2018-05-04 21:40] http404error: I think I like supply last
[2018-05-04 21:40] http404error: supply isn't listed on unit cards anyways in-game so it's not like it has a hard precedent
[2018-05-04 21:41] supergreyman (Greyman): Put supply as last line? 🤔
[2018-05-04 21:41] http404error: **Tarsier** 4:Gold::Red: (10 supply)
Buildtime 2:Time: 
Start of turn: Gain :Attack:
[2018-05-04 21:42] http404error: I think supply belongs on the first line still
[2018-05-04 21:42] http404error: It's important to understanding the context of the unit
[2018-05-04 21:44] supergreyman (Greyman): People have been confused with 4:Red: as :Red: :Red: :Red: :Red:
[2018-05-04 21:44] supergreyman (Greyman): Oops, bad formatting 😛
[2018-05-04 21:44] Deleted User (Deleted User): Actually, the emojis are actually better for things like :Frontline: and :Time:
[2018-05-04 21:45] Deleted User (Deleted User): Now that I think of it
[2018-05-04 21:46] http404error: The emojis are a tough call imo
[2018-05-04 21:46] http404error: :Time:2 is the cleanest
[2018-05-04 21:46] http404error: I think to shorthand :Red::Red::Red::Red: you should always say 4x:Red: instead of 4:Red:

--- Thread (45 messages, 2018-05-04) ---
[2018-05-04 22:05] http404error: (or Gx8 or G^8 or [8G])
[2018-05-04 22:05] liadahlia (Lia): i think [8G] is fairly unambiguous
[2018-05-04 22:06] http404error: This is a very rare case so it's okay to be a little clunky
[2018-05-04 22:06] http404error: That being said, we should have a standard
[2018-05-04 22:06] redrame: [8G] could be 8G but someone didnt read the formatting guide and put the cost in brackets
[2018-05-04 22:06] redrame: I've seen it done before
[2018-05-04 22:06] redrame: very rarely would you have an entire cost in brackets though
[2018-05-04 22:09] http404error: It's too hard to succinctly describe the grammar for abilities
[2018-05-04 22:09] http404error: I was trying to do something like
*ability* text is of the form "pay [cost] and consume [units] to gain [resource] and construct [units]"
[2018-05-04 22:09] http404error: but like, each clause is optional
[2018-05-04 22:10] http404error: so you basically have to define it as a formal grammar
[2018-05-04 22:10] http404error: and then there's other edge cases like Blood Phage's "an additional"
[2018-05-04 22:10] http404error: so for that kind of detail I think the best advice is still "just look at what canon units say on their cards"
[2018-05-04 22:11] Deleted User (Deleted User): **Design Formatting Guide**
Omit any lines that don't apply, anything in *italics* is a variable.

***Unit Name*** (Supply: *supply*) *econ*
*attributes*
When Bought: *econ*
Turn: *attack*, *econ* 
Click: *attack*, *econ*

**Variables**
*attack*: 1-4: :Attack: repeated. 5+ In brackets, number followed by capital X. [\#X]
*attributes*: List: Health (#:Fragile:, :Blocker:, or :Prompt:), Lifespan (:Lifespan:\#), Frontline (:Frontline:), Stamina (:Stamina:\#), ( and/or Time (\#:Time:).
*econ*: List the absolute change of gold, tech (RGBE, :Red::Green::Blue::Energy:), attack (for cost), exhaust, and units. Gold should be a number, Tech can be emoji or letters, Attack as stated in *attack*, exhaust as :Exhaust:\#, unit as the name.
*supply*: Number of unit supply.
[2018-05-04 22:11] http404error: Attack shouldn't always be brackets though
[2018-05-04 22:12] Deleted User (Deleted User): Where should it be without
[2018-05-04 22:12] http404error: up to ":Attack::Attack::Attack::Attack:" at least is the best way to do it
[2018-05-04 22:12] http404error: I don't think you need a numeral until you get to 5 or more
[2018-05-04 22:12] Deleted User (Deleted User): I think even [2X] is more clear and easier to describe as a rule
[2018-05-04 22:12] http404error: That's not what Prismata unit cards do though
[2018-05-04 22:12] http404error: In general we follow the in-game formatting conventions
[2018-05-04 22:13] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): :Lifespan: # and :Time: # , but # :Stamina: ?
[2018-05-04 22:21] http404error: **Design Formatting Guide**

***Name*** *resource cost* (*#* supply)
*additional costs*
*#* hp *fragile/blocker/prompt*
When bought: *ability*
*buildtime/lifespan/stamina/frontline*
Start of turn: *ability*
Click: *ability*

**Notes**
Omit any lines that don't apply
Spells use "Effect:" instead of "When bought:"
Gold is always listed as just a number, optionally followed by the gold symbol (1:Gold:)
All other resources, including attack, are listed by repeating the icon that many times. For amounts of 5 or more, use brackets instead: [8:Green:]
[2018-05-04 22:21] http404error: hmm.. italics are tough to read
[2018-05-04 22:23] http404error: It's also hard to communicate the emoji substitutions
[2018-05-04 22:24] http404error: Prismata puts when bought abilities first
[2018-05-04 22:25] http404error: I might be persuaded to switch lines 2 and 3 though
[2018-05-04 22:25] Deleted User (Deleted User): When comparing the card the information isnt listed in a line by line order
[2018-05-04 22:26] http404error: Well, for anything outside the main text box, yeah
[2018-05-04 22:27] Deleted User (Deleted User): Where do you put it then
[2018-05-04 22:27] http404error: Which item specifically?
[2018-05-04 22:28] http404error: additional costs are just part of when bought now
[2018-05-04 22:28] http404error: I'm just going off of http://prismata.net/units/
[2018-05-04 22:28] Deleted User (Deleted User): We can also just go by wiki
[2018-05-04 22:28] Deleted User (Deleted User): **Arka Sodara**
Ability: gain :Attack::Attack::Attack::Attack:
Supply:  1
Cost: 7:Gold::Blue::Blue::Red:[7X]
Health: 7
Notes: :Prompt: :Blocker:
[2018-05-04 22:29] http404error: I'm not a fan, honestly
[2018-05-04 22:29] http404error: That explains where Notes comes from though
[2018-05-04 22:30] http404error: It's all in the wrong order though, at the very least
[2018-05-04 22:31] http404error: Also takes up so much vertical space
[2018-05-04 22:31] http404error: Also the Attack field is redundant
[2018-05-04 22:36] http404error: I still like the direction we were going better
[2018-05-04 22:36] http404error: **Shiver Yeti** 5:Gold::Red: (10 supply)
2:Health: :Prompt:
Click: :Chill:2

**Venge Cannon** 1:Gold::Green::Green::Green:[3:Drone:] (10 supply)
9:Fragile:
When bought: consume 3 Drones
Start of turn: gain :Attack::Attack:
Click: pay 2:Fragile: and pay :Green::Green::Green::Green: to construct 3 Gauss Charges
[2018-05-04 22:37] http404error: I find that really clean and readable at least, though the gold cost is still a little awkward
[2018-05-04 22:41] http404error: **Name** cost (# supply)
#:Health:/:Fragile: :Blocker:/:Prompt:
When bought: ability
:Time:/:Lifespan:/:Stamina:/:Frontline:
Start of turn: ability
Click: ability

I was trying to do something like
*ability* text is of the form "pay [cost] and consume [units] to gain [resource] and construct [units]"

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: but like, each clause is optional

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: so you basically have to define it as a formal grammar

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: and then there's other edge cases like Blood Phage's "an additional"

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: so for that kind of detail I think the best advice is still "just look at what canon units say on their cards"

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content:
**Design Formatting Guide**
Omit any lines that don't apply, anything in *italics* is a variable.

***Unit Name*** (Supply: *supply*) *econ*
*attributes*
When Bought: *econ*
Turn: *attack*, *econ* 
Click: *attack*, *econ*

**Variables**
*attack*: 1-4: :Attack: repeated. 5+ In brackets, number followed by capital X. [\#X]
*attributes*: List: Health (#:Fragile:, :Blocker:, or :Prompt:), Lifespan (:Lifespan:\#), Frontline (:Frontline:), Stamina (:Stamina:\#), ( and/or Time (\#:Time:).
*econ*: List the absolute change of gold, tech (RGBE, :Red::Green::Blue::Energy:), attack (for cost), exhaust, and units. Gold should be a number, Tech can be emoji or letters, Attack as stated in *attack*, exhaust as :Exhaust:\#, unit as the name.
*supply*: Number of unit supply.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content:
**Design Formatting Guide**

***Name*** *resource cost* (*#* supply)
*additional costs*
*#* hp *fragile/blocker/prompt*
When bought: *ability*
*buildtime/lifespan/stamina/frontline*
Start of turn: *ability*
Click: *ability*

**Notes**
Omit any lines that don't apply
Spells use "Effect:" instead of "When bought:"
Gold is always listed as just a number, optionally followed by the gold symbol (1:Gold:)
All other resources, including attack, are listed by repeating the icon that many times. For amounts of 5 or more, use brackets instead: [8:Green:]
